mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPI inflation above 0.5% monthly growth signals central bank policy tightening, which increases equity discount rates and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPI-based forecasts show no significant correlation with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate weakening supply-side capacity, creating deflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with both INDPRO and HOUST declines (Rank IC < -0.05), or supply indicators fail to predict returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized drop in payroll employment and rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where both PAYEMS and UNRATE exhibit the trigger conditions, or the combined signal fails to outperform baseline in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and consumer spending signals strong demand conditions that support equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to show positive 20d returns in 60% of RSAFS growth periods, or the signal's predictive power drops below 0.05 in 3-year rolling IC tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates economic distress that reduces consumer confidence and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds the trigger threshold, or the signal's IC falls below -0.03 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low Fed funds rates and rising GDP growth signals accommodative policy supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns show no significant correlation with combined FEDFUNDS and GDPC1 signals (p > 0.1), or the interaction effect fails to outperform individual proxies in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase signals strong demand conditions that drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to show positive 20d returns in 60% of GDP growth periods, or the signal's predictive power drops below 0.05 in 3-year rolling IC tests during late-cycle regimes.
